intro.title: Hello, I'm Lain
intro.desc:
Robotics developer and junior undergraduate.
Currently interning at a humanoid robotics company.
Focused on MCU and Linux application development, motion control, RL deployment, and robot perception.
Email: lain-ego@qq.com

item1.title: LocoWiki Robot Locomotion Resource Hub
item1.desc: A one-stop resource hub for robot locomotion, built for ROBOCON preparation, research study, and engineering reference. It aggregates competition rules, university team technical talks, paper reading lists, and mainstream open-source project indexes, with a companion website and Feishu knowledge base.

item2.title: HTDW4438 OpenDog Quadruped
item2.desc: A full-stack open-source quadruped project spanning mechanical design, URDF modeling, Isaac Gym RL training, and sim-to-real deployment. It centers on HTDW4438-OpenDog and works together with HTDW4438-AMP, HTDW4438_HIMloco, and HTDW4438_Isaacgym as one development pipeline.

item3.title: ROBOCON 2025 Legged Robot
item3.desc: Full-stack R&D for the 2025 ROBOCON legged robot. Responsible for carbon-tube mechanical modeling, force control algorithms, and 3D LiDAR SLAM navigation. Led the team to rank 30th in Speed, 31st in Obstacle, and 34th in Cross-Country among 180+ teams.

item4.title: Bionic Lobster Submersible
item4.desc: Buoyancy-controllable multi-DOF underwater robot. Developed the control system (stepper peristaltic pumps, motor/servo drivers, gyroscope fusion, and I2C encoder closed-loop control) and a vectored propulsion model for omnidirectional movement. Handled driver selection and waterproofing. Won the Grand Prize in the Fujian Intelligent Marine Equipment Competition.

item5.title: Intelligent Rice Transplanting and Harvesting Robot
item5.desc: FreeRTOS-based agricultural robot for rice transplanting and harvesting. Controlled DJI 3508 motors for gripper lifting and an omnidirectional chassis, achieving ±5 mm positioning accuracy. Participated in pneumatic design and hardware wiring, supporting dual-robot collaboration.

project1.title: LocoWiki Robot Locomotion Resource Hub
project1.desc:
LocoWiki is a one-stop resource hub for robot locomotion, built for ROBOCON preparation, research study, and engineering reference. It covers core resources for legged-robot development across the full workflow.

Repository contents include:
1) Official competition rules from past seasons;
2) Technical talks from university teams (PPT/PPTX);
3) Paper reading lists and recommended paper PDFs;
4) Mainstream open-source projects, core component drivers, and learning knowledge-base directories.

The companion website locowiki.github.io provides module navigation, commonly used tool links, and Feishu knowledge-base entry points, along with a QQ group for developer and team communication.

project2.title: HTDW4438 OpenDog Quadruped
project2.desc:
HTDW4438 OpenDog is a full-stack open-source quadruped project focused on reliable real-world deployment of RL-based locomotion. The workflow covers mechanical design, URDF modeling, simulation training, policy export, and on-robot validation.

Repository layout highlights:
1) 1.Hardware for mechanical assets, URDF, and meshes;
2) 2.Software for livelybot_sdk and hardware control scripts;
3) 3.Document / 4.Paper for BOM, manuals, and technical references.

The training and deployment chain is driven by three related repositories:
1) HTDW4438-AMP for motion-prior RL training and policy iteration;
2) HTDW4438_HIMloco for locomotion policy adaptation and control optimization;
3) HTDW4438_Isaacgym for large-scale GPU simulation, playback, and policy export.

Together they form a practical end-to-end loop: mechanical design/URDF -> simulation training -> sim-to-real deployment.

project3.title: ROBOCON 2025 Legged Robot Challenge
project3.desc:
ROBOCON is a major collegiate robotics competition that places high demands on mechanical reliability, electronic-control stability, and vision accuracy.

In the 2025 season, I was responsible for nearly the full-stack R&D of our first legged-robot team, covering mechanical modeling, motion control, and autonomous navigation. Using a carbon-tube assembly approach inspired by agricultural drone structures, force control algorithms, and 3D LiDAR mapping and navigation, our team ranked 30th in the Speed Race, 31st in the Obstacle Race, and 34th in the Cross-Country Race at ROBOCON 2025 in Jiangyin among over 180 teams, winning National Second Prize in all three categories.

project4.title: Bionic Lobster Submersible
project4.desc:
This project is a buoyancy-controllable multi-DOF lobster-inspired underwater robot.

On the software side, I was responsible for control-system development and designed a low-level embedded control framework integrating stepper peristaltic pumps, motor/servo drivers, gyroscope data processing, and I2C encoder feedback for closed-loop control of 360-degree servos. I also designed a vectored propulsion model for the irregular multi-rotor structure to achieve omnidirectional movement. On the hardware side, I handled driver selection, buck-converter circuit design, and waterproofing.

The project won the Grand Prize in the Underwater Robot Track at the 2024 Fujian University Student Intelligent Marine Equipment Design Competition. It also represented Fujian University of Technology at the 2024 World Maritime Equipment Conference, receiving coverage from media outlets such as Xinhua News Agency and Guangming Daily.

project5.title: Intelligent Rice Transplanting and Harvesting Robot
project5.desc:
Based on the FreeRTOS real-time operating system, I developed the control system for an integrated agricultural robot for rice transplanting and harvesting.

By controlling DJI 3508 motors, I completed functional debugging of the gripper lifting mechanism, omnidirectional wheeled chassis, and omnidirectional positioning system, achieving gripper positioning accuracy within ±5 mm. I also participated in pneumatic-system design and hardware wiring, supporting collaborative operation with another autonomous robot to complete tasks efficiently.

item1.title: BRS Parallel-Link Quadruped Robot
item1.desc: Project 'BLACK☆ROCK SHOOTER': a full-stack quadruped robot built around a parallel-link leg mechanism. The complete solution is open-sourced and competed in the 2025 ROBOCON National Competition.

item2.title: SliverWolf Desktop Serial-Link Quadruped
item2.desc: A direct-drive, position-controlled serial-link quadruped using 9g micro servos. Features IMU integration, voice control, multiple gaits, and collaborative object fetching with a robotic arm.

item3.title: BionicLobster ROV
item3.desc: Open-source bionic lobster underwater robot project, including the control system, vectored propulsion model, and hardware design for a buoyancy-controllable multi-DOF underwater robot.

item4.title: ROBOCON 2024 R1 Robot
item4.desc: Low-level control repository for the ROBOCON 2024 R1 robot, including mechanical schemes, omnidirectional chassis kinematics, and task mechanism logic.

item5.title: 3508 Legged-Robot Drive Test Platform
item5.desc: A quadruped drive solution based on DJI 3508 brushless geared motors, used in multiple ROBOCON National Competitions from 2022 to 2025.

item6.title: HTDW4438 HIMloco
item6.desc: HIMloco locomotion control and deployment project for HTDW4438, covering policy transfer and real-robot validation workflows.

item7.title: HTDW4438 OpenDog Deployment
item7.desc: Real-robot control and deployment code for HTDW4438, including mechanical design, control algorithm implementation, and system integration testing.

item8.title: ROBOCON2024-PPS
item8.desc: Code repository for the ROBOCON 2024 PPS-related robot project, including competition task logic and control implementations.

item9.title: LocoWiki
item9.desc: A lightweight open-source wiki and knowledge management project focused on structured note organization, fast retrieval, and long-term knowledge growth.

event11.title: Internship at Qianxing Intelligence
event11.desc: Responsible for motion control and human-robot interaction R&D for humanoid robots.

event10.desc: Launched the LocoWiki resource hub for robot competition preparation, research learning, and development reference, bringing together competition rules, technical sharing, paper reading lists, and high-quality open-source projects in one place.

event6.desc: Responsible for full-stack R&D of the legged robot. Ranked 30th nationally in the Speed Race, 31st in the Obstacle Race, and 34th in the Cross-Country Race, securing National Second Prize in all three categories.
